email_summary: Strauss ordered 40 tons of standard-grade cocoa from Ivoire Cacao Export for Q1 2026, with an agreed 14-business-day delivery window. Severe port congestion at Abidjan caused the shipment to arrive approximately 10–11 weeks late (mid-April instead of mid-February), and the delivered quantity was 38.5 tons due to a pre-loading quality failure. A $4,275 credit for the 1.5-ton shortfall was promised on the Q2 invoice. Unresolved as of June 2026: no formal written price review has been submitted by the supplier for either the Q1 rate ($2,850/ton) or the proposed Q2 rate ($3,050/ton), both of which exceed the contract base price of $2,650/ton without a documented amendment, and Strauss has set a June 10 deadline for formal documentation before placing the Q2 order.

contract_terms.payment_terms: Net 30 from bill of lading date
contract_terms.key_penalty: Delivery delay beyond agreed window incurs 1% of shipment value per week delayed, capped at 10%.

open_issues.description: Contract STR-COCOA-2025-002 had a renewal date of 2026-05-31 and there is no evidence in the email thread or contract file of a renewal or replacement agreement having been signed. Strauss is currently operating without an active contract covering Q2 and beyond.
open_issues.source_ref: Contract STR-COCOA-2025-002, Effective Date / Term fields; no renewal email found in thread

open_issues.description: The Q1 rate of $2,850/ton quoted by Amara Koffi on 2026-01-19 exceeds the contract base price of $2,650/ton by $200/ton, and no written price review or amendment has been executed by either party as required by the contract's quarterly review mechanism.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-01-19 from Amara Koffi; Contract STR-COCOA-2025-002 Pricing clause; Email 2026-05-06 from David Cohen

open_issues.description: The Q2 proposed rate of $3,050/ton quoted by Amara Koffi on 2026-03-10 exceeds the contract base price of $2,650/ton by $400/ton, and despite multiple requests from David Cohen (2026-04-16, 2026-05-06, 2026-06-01) no formal written price review request with ICCO benchmark reference has been received from the supplier.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-03-10 from Amara Koffi; Email 2026-06-01 from David Cohen; Contract STR-COCOA-2025-002 Pricing clause

open_issues.description: The Q1 shipment arrived approximately 10–11 weeks after order confirmation, far exceeding the contracted 14-business-day window. Under the contract's penalty clause, Strauss is entitled to claim up to 10% of shipment value (capped) for the weeks of delay, but no penalty has been raised, calculated, or agreed upon in the thread.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-01-19 (14-day commitment); Email 2026-04-16 (arrival confirmed); Contract STR-COCOA-2025-002 Penalty Clauses

open_issues.description: As of the email dated 2026-06-01, Strauss has set a June 10 deadline for receipt of formal price review documentation before placing the Q2 order (35–40 tons), but no response or documentation has been received from Ivoire Cacao Export.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-06-01 from David Cohen to Amara Koffi and JB Ouattara

commodity_benchmark: The ICCO cocoa spot price has risen sharply from approximately $3,245/ton in late March 2026 to $4,143/ton as of mid-June 2026, meaning both the Q1 rate ($2,850) and proposed Q2 rate ($3,050) are now well below the current market benchmark, which significantly weakens Strauss's leverage to reject a price increase but does not relieve the supplier of the obligation to follow the contract's formal price review process.
heads_up: The contract expired on 2026-05-31 with no renewal on file, so Strauss is currently uncontracted; before discussing Q2 pricing or volume, the priority is to execute a new or extended agreement. Separately, the delayed Q1 delivery almost certainly triggers the 1% per week penalty clause (capped at 10% of shipment value), which Strauss has not yet formally claimed — this is a tangible financial recovery point to raise in the meeting.
negotiation_brief: The relationship is operationally troubled: the supplier's commercial and logistics contacts are siloed, the Q1 shipment arrived roughly 10 weeks late (triggering an unclaimed delay penalty worth up to 10% of the ~$109,675 shipment value, i.e. up to ~$10,968), and the contract lapsed on 2026-05-31 with no signed renewal. On price, Strauss holds the process high ground — neither the Q1 rate ($2,850) nor the Q2 proposal ($3,050) was put through the mandatory written review mechanism — but the market has moved sharply against Strauss: cocoa is now trading above $4,100/ton, making the supplier's $3,050 ask look modest by comparison. Walking in, Strauss should bundle three things: (1) execute a contract renewal immediately or all orders are on hold, (2) demand the formal ICCO-referenced price review letter as a condition of Q2 order placement per the contract's amendment clause, and (3) formally raise the delivery delay penalty as a credit or offset against any agreed price step-up — this is the primary negotiating chip against the supplier's market-price argument.

financial_signals.delivery_reliability_score: Poor — repeated delays or unresolved delivery issue

benchmark.source: ICE Cocoa Futures (CC=F) via Yahoo Finance

raw_emails.subject: Q1 Cocoa Pricing + Delivery Schedule
raw_emails.body:
Hi Amara,

Hope you're well. Can you send over your Q1 pricing for the standard grade cocoa, and confirm the delivery window we discussed for the February shipment (40 tons)?

Thanks,
David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q1 Cocoa Pricing + Delivery Schedule
raw_emails.body:
Hello David, sorry for delay, was traveling.

Q1 price is $2,850/ton for standard grade. Please note that all pricing from Ivoire Cacao Export is denominated in USD. The exchange rate applied to your invoice is the prevailing rate on the date of invoice issuance, not the date of order.

For delivery — our standard dispatch-to-arrival window is 14 business days from order confirmation. Please check with Jean-Baptiste in operations for the specific schedule, he handles logistics. Let me know if you want to proceed and I send formal quote letter.

Best,
Amara

raw_emails.subject: Delivery confirmation - Feb shipment (40t)
raw_emails.body:
Hi Jean-Baptiste,

Amara mentioned you handle logistics. We're confirming the Q1 order — 40 tons standard grade. She says the standard window is 14 business days from order confirmation. We need the shipment on site before February 20 for production planning. Can you confirm this is achievable and send a schedule?

Thanks,
David

raw_emails.subject: Re: Delivery confirmation - Feb shipment (40t)
raw_emails.body:
David

port congestion abidjan this month, vessel delay. we are now tracking approximately 26 business days from order confirmation date due to the situation at port. realistic arrival now late february or early march. will update closer.

sorry

jb

raw_emails.subject: RE: Delivery — this is a problem
raw_emails.body:
JB,

Amara confirmed 14 business days at order. We are now looking at 26 business days — that is nearly double the committed window and it puts us past our production deadline.

I need a firm updated date this week. Is there any option to expedite — partial shipment, alternative vessel?

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: delivery update
raw_emails.body:
David, I understand. I spoke with JB. The port situation is outside our control but I know it does not help you.

He says earliest realistic vessel now is February 10 departure from Abidjan, so arrival at your port around March 5-10. No option to split — the batch is one lot.

Amara

raw_emails.subject: Following up - shipment status
raw_emails.body:
JB,

We are past the March 5-10 window and I have no update. This is now six weeks past the original 14-business-day commitment. Can you give me a firm date this week?

David

raw_emails.subject: Updated pricing for upcoming order
raw_emails.body:
Hi David, hope all is good.

Quick note — cocoa market has moved a lot since January. For any new order after this batch the price will be $3,050/ton, reflecting current ICCO benchmark plus our margin. Let me know if you want to lock the current order at old price before it ships.

Amara

raw_emails.subject: Re: Updated pricing + shipment still missing
raw_emails.body:
Amara,

The current order hasn't even shipped yet. JB said early March and we're still waiting. Can we get clarity on both — when does this order actually arrive, and is the new pricing only for future orders or does it touch what is already contracted?

David

raw_emails.subject: shipment update
raw_emails.body:
david

container left port last week, shd reach you mid april. note final loaded qty was 38.5t not 40t — two pallets failed quality check at our end before loading. will credit difference on next invoice.

jb

raw_emails.subject: Re: shipment arrived — delivery confirmed
raw_emails.body:
JB,

Confirming 38.5t arrived today and passed incoming inspection. Quality is fine.

On the short shipment — you mentioned a credit on the next invoice for the 1.5t shortfall. At Q1 contract price of $2,850/ton that is $4,275. Please make sure this appears on the next invoice.

Also — Amara mentioned $3,050/ton for Q2. Our contract (signed June 2025) sets a base price with a quarterly review mechanism — price adjustments require written confirmation from both sides. Neither the January quote ($2,850) nor the proposed Q2 quote has gone through that process. I need a formal written price review request before we can agree to anything above contract base.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 order + credit
raw_emails.body:
Hi David,

Glad shipment arrived ok. Credit of $4,275 will appear on the Q2 invoice, Jean-Baptiste will raise it.

For Q2 — yes, new price is $3,050/ton. This is not negotiable, the market is where it is. When do you want to place Q2 order? We need at least 6 weeks lead time.

Amara

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 order — formal price review required
raw_emails.body:
Amara,

I need to be clear. Our contract sets a base price of $2,650/ton with a quarterly review mechanism. Price adjustments require written confirmation from both parties. Neither the $2,850 Q1 rate nor the $3,050 Q2 proposal has gone through that process formally.

I am not saying we won't pay a fair market price. But we need to follow the contract process. Please send a formal written price review request with the ICCO benchmark reference so we can process this on our end.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 order
raw_emails.body:
David,

Sorry for slow reply, I was at the Salon du Cacao in Paris.

I understand what you say about the contract process. Honestly I do not have a copy of the contract here — Jean-Baptiste handles administration. I will ask him to check and send something formal. But we cannot wait too long, the Q2 harvest window is short.

Amara

raw_emails.subject: Q2 — still waiting on formal price review
raw_emails.body:
Amara, JB,

It has been three weeks since I asked for a formal price review request and I have not received anything. We want to place the Q2 order — 35-40 tons — but I cannot authorize payment above the contract base price without a written amendment.

If I do not hear back with the formal documentation by June 10, we will either place at contract price or pause until we can align.

David

raw_contracts.supplier_01_ivoire_cacao.md:
# Contract Summary — Ivoire Cacao Export

**Contract ID:** STR-COCOA-2025-002
**Supplier:** Ivoire Cacao Export (Côte d'Ivoire)
**Category:** Cocoa — Standard grade beans
**Effective Date:** 2025-06-01
**Term:** 12 months
**Renewal Date:** 2026-05-31

## Payment Terms
Net 30 from bill of lading date.

## Volume Commitment
Minimum 35 tons/quarter, maximum 50 tons/quarter. Volumes below minimum
for two consecutive quarters trigger a contract review clause.

## Pricing
Base price: $2,650/ton at signing, subject to quarterly review against
ICCO benchmark. Either party may request a price review with 15 days
notice; agreed adjustments require written confirmation from both sides.

## Penalty Clauses
- Delivery delay beyond agreed window: 1% of shipment value per week
  delayed, capped at 10%.
- Short-shipment (delivered quantity below confirmed PO quantity): credit
  issued on next invoice for the shortfall at contract price.

## Notes
Cross-reference against supplier 1's email thread: the January quote
($2,850/ton) and March quote ($3,050/ton) both exceed the $2,650/ton
contract base price without a documented written price review on file in
the email thread, this is a real gap the prototype should flag (price
quoted informally by sales contact does not match what's on record in the
contract, and no formal amendment is visible).
